scenario: Quartz Bank notices that its Camunda 8.8 job workers see an extra 2 seconds of activation delay on a large cluster during quiet periods, even with long polling enabled across 8 worker instances. A senior engineer wants the official recommendation for cutting this delay.
question: Which Camunda 8.8 worker performance best practice does the page recommend to reduce job activation latency?

options.text: Enable job streaming so workers no longer have to balance aggressive polling of every partition against minimizing cluster impact, which on large clusters can otherwise add a noticeable delay in the order of seconds

Disable long polling on all workers and shorten the poll interval to 10 ms; long polling forces every worker to wait for the full server-side timeout before receiving newly activated jobs from the broker

Increase the broker partition count to one partition per active worker; job activation delay is caused by partitions being assigned to multiple workers and is independent of the polling mechanism used
Switch workers to short polling and disable batched activation; fetching one job per request reduces the per-job activation overhead and is the recommended Camunda 8.8 performance pattern

a.text: Correct. The Performance best-practices section recommends enabling job streaming to reduce latency to a maximum, since with long polling workers must periodically poll every partition and balance aggression against cluster load, which on large clusters can add a noticeable delay in the order of seconds.

b.text: Incorrect. The best practice keeps long polling as a baseline and adds job streaming on top of it; tightening short-polling intervals is not the documented fix.

c.text: Incorrect. Job activation delay on large clusters is described as a polling-pattern problem solved by streaming, not a partition-to-worker mapping problem.

d.text: Incorrect. The best practice does not recommend disabling batched activation or moving to short polling for performance.

explanation: Camunda 8.8 best practice recommends enabling job streaming on workers to cut job activation latency, since long polling on large clusters forces a tradeoff between polling aggression and cluster load that can add seconds of delay.
